input.plan:
# Add `cli` as a new extension type

## Context

AgentCenter currently ships four extension categories — `skills`, `mcp`, `slash`, `plugins` — codified by the `extension_category` Postgres enum. We want a fifth: `cli`, a publisher-uploaded extension whose payload is an executable or installable package, parallel in every way to the existing four but installed to a different location on the user's machine. The existing surface area is fortunately well-factored for this: the manifest spec is already `[install.<agent>]`-keyed per category, the approvals matrix is keyed by `extensionCategory`, and the i18n labels live in two flat dictionaries. The bulk of the work is a mechanical parallel addition — the only design decision is the install destination, which we pin to `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` to mirror skills/plugins (directory drop, same uninstall semantics, user can symlink to PATH themselves).

This is additive to the frozen `/api/v1` contract — the `category` field gains one new accepted value. Existing CLI clients on older versions will see `cli` in API responses; they'll either skip unknown values or fail validation. The CLI must be released in lockstep so users can actually install `cli`-type extensions, but the schema change itself is non-breaking per the `/api/v1` rules.

## Approach

Five-part change, landed as one feature branch with conventional-commit phase scope `feat(p?-cli-type)`:

1. **Enum + validator fanout** — every single source of truth for the four-value enum gets a fifth value
2. **Manifest spec + bundle layer** — add a `[install.<agent>]` example for `cli`
3. **CLI installer** — new `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` mirroring `skills.ts`; install/uninstall branches in `installers/index.ts`
4. **Seed data** — approval-admin seed rows so the new tab in `/admin/approvals` isn't empty; optional demo extension rows so the listing page has content
5. **UI surfaces** — i18n labels (en/zh), the legacy-category redirect map, and a `/cli` listing route mirroring the four existing ones

DB migration: adding a value to a pg enum is a single `ALTER TYPE … ADD VALUE`. Drizzle Kit generates this from the schema diff — no manual SQL.

## Critical files to modify

### Enum / type single sources of truth (touch every one of these — drift will compile-error)

- `shared/db/schema/extension.ts:29-34` — append `"cli"` to `extensionCategoryEnum`. Triggers a Drizzle migration via `bun run db:generate`.
- `shared/types.ts:5` — append `"cli"` to `ExtensionCategory` union.
- `shared/validators/manifest.ts:23` — append `"cli"` to the `z.enum` in `ExtensionManifestCore.category`.
- `shared/validators/filters.ts:3` — append `"cli"` to `CATEGORIES`.
- `shared/validators/approvals.ts:25` — append `"cli"` to the `ExtensionCategory` z.enum (this is a second copy of the same idea, used by the approvals layer; both must stay in sync — flag for future consolidation but not in this PR).
- `shared/data/approval-admins.ts:22` — append `"cli"` to `SeedExtensionCategory`.

### Category-branched logic (extend the switch with a `cli` case)

- `server/utils/publish.ts:22-34` — `defaultClassification()`: add `case "cli": return { funcCat: "tools", subCat: "cli" }`. Pick `tools/cli` as the natural taxonomy slot.
- `cli/src/installers/index.ts:8-25` — both `installExtension` and `uninstallExtension` switches: add `case "cli": return installCli(...) / uninstallCli(...)`.
- `app/middleware/legacy-categories.global.ts:1-7` — append `cli: "/cli"` to `CATEGORY_REDIRECTS`.

### i18n labels (two files, mirror keys)

- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` — every place that enumerates the four categories needs a `cli` key. From the grep:
  - `nav.search.{cat}.label` + `nav.search.{cat}.searchPlaceholder` (around line 110–124 in en.json)
  - `nav.{skills,mcpServers,slashCommands,plugins}` top-level labels (around line 145–148) — add a `cli` sibling
  - `publish.options.category.*` (around line 336–340)
  - The admin approvals matrix labels (around line 587–590)
  - Any home/landing copy that lists category counts (search for `discoveryHeading` neighbours)
  - Suggested labels: en `"CLI"`, zh `"命令行工具"` — confirm zh wording with user if uncertain.

### Manifest spec docs

- `docs/manifest-spec.md:48-95` — add a fifth subsection between Plugins and Path-tokens:
  ```toml
  **CLI tools** (`category = "cli"`):

  [install.claude]
  cli = "~/.claude/cli/{slug}"
  ```
  Note that the directory may contain an executable, a package, or both — the CLI does a directory drop and the publisher's `postInstall.message` can guide users on PATH wiring or `npm link`.

### Approval-admin seed rows

- `shared/data/approval-admins.ts:36-91` — at minimum:
  - One company-tier `all/*` row (mirror line 41–44 pattern, `reviewerEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev"`) so super-admin coverage extends to the new tab.
  - Two macro rows so the new tab isn't visibly empty when an admin opens it — e.g., `cli × company × macro × softDev → ben` and `cli × company × macro × cloud → cory`.
  - One PL `all/*` row (e.g., wireless → dao) so PL queues route.
  - No need to seed a pending request unless you also want the queue to light up on `bun run db:seed`.

### Optional demo extensions

- `shared/data/extensions.ts` — add 1–2 entries with `category: "cli"` so the new `/cli` listing has content on a fresh DB. Mirror the shape of the existing `category: "plugins"` entries (lines 72, 168, 303). Skip if you'd rather the seed list stay lean.

## New files to create

### CLI installer

- `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` — mirror `cli/src/installers/skills.ts` exactly; only difference is `const CLI_DIR = join(homedir(), ".claude", "cli")`. The directory-drop + `unzipSync` + per-entry write pattern is identical, including the rollback-on-error and `listInstalledSlugs` helper.

### Listing page

- `app/pages/cli.vue` — mirror the existing `skills.vue` / `mcp.vue` / `commands.vue` / `plugins.vue` listing pages. These wrap the same `ExtensionListing` component with a fixed `category` prop. (If the existing four are colocated under a shared component, just register the new route there — Explore agent flagged `ExtensionListing.vue:~32` but didn't enumerate page files; verify which pattern is in use before writing this file.) The MCP panorama link branch in `ExtensionListing.vue:~32` is mcp-specific; no `cli` branch needed.

## Migration

`bun run db:generate` after editing `shared/db/schema/extension.ts` produces a migration like:

```sql
ALTER TYPE "extension_category" ADD VALUE 'cli';
```

`ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` cannot run inside a transaction in Postgres < 12 but is fine on Supabase's Postgres 15+; Drizzle Kit handles this correctly. Run `bun run db:migrate` to apply locally.

## Reused functions / utilities (don't reinvent)

- **Install primitive**: `installSkill` at `cli/src/installers/skills.ts:9` — copy its body verbatim into `installCli` with the dest dir changed. Same rollback semantics, same `unzipSync`, same dir-entry skip.
- **Category z.enum in `ExtensionManifestCore`** at `shared/validators/manifest.ts:14` is `.pick()`'d by `BundleManifestSchema` at line 84 — extending the parent automatically extends the child; no bundle-schema edit needed.
- **Default classification fallback**: `defaultClassification()` at `server/utils/publish.ts:22` already has a `default` branch returning `tools/general`; if you'd rather not pick a `cli`-specific subCat, removing the new case lets it fall through to that default. Choose explicitly for taxonomy filter UX.

## Open question to confirm before commit

- **subCat for `cli`**: I've proposed `tools/cli` in the default classification. If you prefer it fall through to `tools/general` (matching the current skills/plugins default), say so. This affects the FUNC_TAXONOMY filter rail behaviour only — not the listing route.

## Branch, commit split, PR (per CLAUDE.md §13)

1. **Branch**: `git checkout -b feat/cli-extension-type` off the current `main`.
2. **Commits** (three, in order, each runs `bun run validate` locally before pushing):
   1. `feat(p?-cli-type)!: add cli extension category` — schema enum + types + validators + migration. The `!` is conservative: technically additive on `/api/v1`, but downstream CLIs that hard-validate the enum will reject `cli` rows until upgraded. I'll drop the `!` if you prefer, since the contract widening is forward-compatible for clients that ignore unknown values.
   2. `feat(p?-cli-type): wire cli installer and manifest spec` — `cli/src/installers/cli.ts`, the installers/index switch, and `docs/manifest-spec.md`.
   3. `feat(p?-cli-type): seed and surface cli category in UI` — approval-admin seed rows, i18n labels (en+zh), `legacy-categories.global.ts`, `app/pages/cli.vue`, optional demo extensions.
3. **PR**: `gh pr create` against `main`. PR body summarises the additive contract change, lists the touched single-source-of-truth files, and links the manifest-spec update. No `Co-Authored-By` trailer and no Claude-generated footer (per CLAUDE.md).
4. **Push and stop**: do not merge; wait for human review per the phase-boundary checkpoint rule.

## Verification

End-to-end on a clean checkout:

1. `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate` — migration applies cleanly, no diff drift.
2. `SEED_PASSWORD=test bun run db:seed` — seed succeeds; the four existing approval-admin rows plus the new `cli` rows all insert.
3. `bun run dev` and visit `/cli` — listing renders with the i18n label, optional demo extensions visible if seeded.
4. Sign in as amy (super-admin) and visit `/admin/approvals` — the matrix shows a `CLI` tab with the seeded admin rows visible.
5. Run the publish wizard end-to-end with `category: "cli"`, upload a small zip, submit for review. Confirm the draft → scanning → ready state transition works (no category-specific branches in `extensions-state.ts` to break).
6. From a separate terminal: `cd cli && bun run build && ./dist/bin/agentcenter install <slug>` against the local dev server. Confirm files land in `~/.claude/cli/<slug>/` and `agentcenter list` shows the entry. Then `agentcenter uninstall <slug>` and confirm the directory is gone.
7. `bun run validate` (typecheck + lint + unit + e2e per docs/plan.md §13) passes — coverage thresholds hold; the new enum value should not regress existing tests, and the few that hard-code the four-value list (`shared/validators/manifest.test.ts:18`, `shared/search/query.test.ts:22`, `shared/validators/approvals.test.ts:186+`) keep working because they assert with `skills`, not with `.length === 4`.   - One company-tier `all/*` row (mirror line 41–44 pattern, `reviewerEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev"`) so super-admin coverage extends to the new tab.

### Optional demo extensions

- `shared/data/extensions.ts` — add 1–2 entries with `category: "cli"` so the new `/cli` listing has content on a fresh DB. Mirror the shape of the existing `category: "plugins"` entries (lines 72, 168, 303). Skip if you'd rather the seed list stay lean.

### CLI installer

- `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` — mirror `cli/src/installers/skills.ts` exactly; only difference is `const CLI_DIR = join(homedir(), ".claude", "cli")`. The directory-drop + `unzipSync` + per-entry write pattern is identical, including the rollback-on-error and `listInstalledSlugs` helper.

### Listing page

- `app/pages/cli.vue` — mirror the existing `skills.vue` / `mcp.vue` / `commands.vue` / `plugins.vue` listing pages. These wrap the same `ExtensionListing` component with a fixed `category` prop. ## Migration

`bun run db:generate` after editing `shared/db/schema/extension.ts` produces a migration like:

```sql
ALTER TYPE "extension_category" ADD VALUE 'cli';
```

`ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` cannot run inside a transaction in Postgres < 12 but is fine on Supabase's Postgres 15+; Drizzle Kit handles this correctly. Run `bun run db:migrate` to apply locally.

## Reused functions / utilities (don't reinvent)

- **Install primitive**: `installSkill` at `cli/src/installers/skills.ts:9` — copy its body verbatim into `installCli` with the dest dir changed. Same rollback semantics, same `unzipSync`, same dir-entry skip.
- **Category z.enum in `ExtensionManifestCore`** at `shared/validators/manifest.ts:14` is `.pick()`'d by `BundleManifestSchema` at line 84 — extending the parent automatically extends the child; no bundle-schema edit needed.
- **Default classification fallback**: `defaultClassification()` at `server/utils/publish.ts:22` already has a `default` branch returning `tools/general`; if you'd rather not pick a `cli`-specific subCat, removing the new case lets it fall through to that default. Choose explicitly for taxonomy filter UX.

- **subCat for `cli`**: I've proposed `tools/cli` in the default classification. If you prefer it fall through to `tools/general` (matching the current skills/plugins default), say so. This affects the FUNC_TAXONOMY filter rail behaviour only — not the listing route.

## Branch, commit split, PR (per CLAUDE.md §13)

1. **Branch**: `git checkout -b feat/cli-extension-type` off the current `main`.
2. **Commits** (three, in order, each runs `bun run validate` locally before pushing):
   1. `feat(p?-cli-type)!: add cli extension category` — schema enum + types + validators + migration. The `!` is conservative: technically additive on `/api/v1`, but downstream CLIs that hard-validate the enum will reject `cli` rows until upgraded. I'll drop the `!` if you prefer, since the contract widening is forward-compatible for clients that ignore unknown values.
   2. `feat(p?-cli-type): wire cli installer and manifest spec` — `cli/src/installers/cli.ts`, the installers/index switch, and `docs/manifest-spec.md`.
   3. `feat(p?-cli-type): seed and surface cli category in UI` — approval-admin seed rows, i18n labels (en+zh), `legacy-categories.global.ts`, `app/pages/cli.vue`, optional demo extensions.

4. **Push and stop**: do not merge; wait for human review per the phase-boundary checkpoint rule.

1. `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate` — migration applies cleanly, no diff drift.
2. `SEED_PASSWORD=test bun run db:seed` — seed succeeds; the four existing approval-admin rows plus the new `cli` rows all insert.
3. `bun run dev` and visit `/cli` — listing renders with the i18n label, optional demo extensions visible if seeded.
4. Sign in as amy (super-admin) and visit `/admin/approvals` — the matrix shows a `CLI` tab with the seeded admin rows visible.
5. Run the publish wizard end-to-end with `category: "cli"`, upload a small zip, submit for review. Confirm the draft → scanning → ready state transition works (no category-specific branches in `extensions-state.ts` to break).

Add at least one test row covering `category: "cli"` to `filters.test.ts` and `manifest.test.ts` to lock the contract.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Add `cli` as a new extension type

## Context

AgentCenter currently ships four extension categories — `skills`, `mcp`, `slash`, `plugins` — codified by the `extension_category` Postgres enum. We want a fifth: `cli`, a publisher-uploaded extension whose payload is an executable or installable package, parallel in every way to the existing four but installed to a different location on the user's machine. The existing surface area is fortunately well-factored for this: the manifest spec is already `[install.<agent>]`-keyed per category, the approvals matrix is keyed by `extensionCategory`, and the i18n labels live in two flat dictionaries. The bulk of the work is a mechanical parallel addition — the only design decision is the install destination, which we pin to `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` to mirror skills/plugins (directory drop, same uninstall semantics, user can symlink to PATH themselves).

This is additive to the frozen `/api/v1` contract — the `category` field gains one new accepted value. Existing CLI clients on older versions will see `cli` in API responses; they'll either skip unknown values or fail validation. The CLI must be released in lockstep so users can actually install `cli`-type extensions, but the schema change itself is non-breaking per the `/api/v1` rules.

## Approach

Five-part change, landed as one feature branch with conventional-commit phase scope `feat(p?-cli-type)`:

1. **Enum + validator fanout** — every single source of truth for the four-value enum gets a fifth value
2. **Manifest spec + bundle layer** — add a `[install.<agent>]` example for `cli`
3. **CLI installer** — new `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` mirroring `skills.ts`; install/uninstall branches in `installers/index.ts`
4. **Seed data** — approval-admin seed rows so the new tab in `/admin/approvals` isn't empty; optional demo extension rows so the listing page has content
5. **UI surfaces** — i18n labels (en/zh), the legacy-category redirect map, and a `/cli` listing route mirroring the four existing ones

DB migration: adding a value to a pg enum is a single `ALTER TYPE … ADD VALUE`. Drizzle Kit generates this from the schema diff — no manual SQL.

## Critical files to modify

### Enum / type single sources of truth (touch every one of these — drift will compile-error)

- `shared/db/schema/extension.ts:29-34` — append `"cli"` to `extensionCategoryEnum`. Triggers a Drizzle migration via `bun run db:generate`.
- `shared/types.ts:5` — append `"cli"` to `ExtensionCategory` union.
- `shared/validators/manifest.ts:23` — append `"cli"` to the `z.enum` in `ExtensionManifestCore.category`.
- `shared/validators/filters.ts:3` — append `"cli"` to `CATEGORIES`.
- `shared/validators/approvals.ts:25` — append `"cli"` to the `ExtensionCategory` z.enum (this is a second copy of the same idea, used by the approvals layer; both must stay in sync — flag for future consolidation but not in this PR).
- `shared/data/approval-admins.ts:22` — append `"cli"` to `SeedExtensionCategory`.

### Category-branched logic (extend the switch with a `cli` case)

- `server/utils/publish.ts:22-34` — `defaultClassification()`: add `case "cli": return { funcCat: "tools", subCat: "cli" }`. Pick `tools/cli` as the natural taxonomy slot.
- `cli/src/installers/index.ts:8-25` — both `installExtension` and `uninstallExtension` switches: add `case "cli": return installCli(...) / uninstallCli(...)`.
- `app/middleware/legacy-categories.global.ts:1-7` — append `cli: "/cli"` to `CATEGORY_REDIRECTS`.

### i18n labels (two files, mirror keys)

- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` — every place that enumerates the four categories needs a `cli` key. From the grep:
  - `nav.search.{cat}.label` + `nav.search.{cat}.searchPlaceholder` (around line 110–124 in en.json)
  - `nav.{skills,mcpServers,slashCommands,plugins}` top-level labels (around line 145–148) — add a `cli` sibling
  - `publish.options.category.*` (around line 336–340)
  - The admin approvals matrix labels (around line 587–590)
  - Any home/landing copy that lists category counts (search for `discoveryHeading` neighbours)
  - Suggested labels: en `"CLI"`, zh `"命令行工具"` — confirm zh wording with user if uncertain.

### Manifest spec docs

- `docs/manifest-spec.md:48-95` — add a fifth subsection between Plugins and Path-tokens:
  ```toml
  **CLI tools** (`category = "cli"`):

  [install.claude]
  cli = "~/.claude/cli/{slug}"
  ```
  Note that the directory may contain an executable, a package, or both — the CLI does a directory drop and the publisher's `postInstall.message` can guide users on PATH wiring or `npm link`.

1	import { z } from "zod"
2	
3	import {
4	  ALL_L1_KEYS,
5	  ALL_L2_KEYS,
6	  type CategoryLevel,
7	} from "~~/shared/taxonomy"
8	
9	const L1_KEY_SET = new Set<string>(ALL_L1_KEYS)
10	const L2_KEY_SET = new Set<string>(ALL_L2_KEYS)
11	
12	// `SUB_CAT_KEY_LIST` is the source of truth for the publisher subCat picker
13	// and the request-row validator — same set as `ALL_L1_KEYS`, just re-exported
14	// under the historical name so the matrix UI and the publish dialog stay
15	// in lockstep.
16	export const SUB_CAT_KEY_LIST: readonly string[] = ALL_L1_KEYS
17	
18	export const OfficialTier = z.enum(["productLine", "company"])
19	export type OfficialTier = z.infer<typeof OfficialTier>
20	
21	export const SubCatKey = z.string().refine((v) => L1_KEY_SET.has(v), {
22	  message: "must be a key from FUNC_TAXONOMY",
23	})
24	
25	export const ExtensionCategory = z.enum(["skills", "mcp", "slash", "plugins"])
26	export type ExtensionCategory = z.infer<typeof ExtensionCategory>
27	
28	export const AdminCategoryLevel = z.enum(["all", "macro", "micro"])
29	export type AdminCategoryLevel = CategoryLevel
30	
31	// Product-line ids are seeded text PKs in kebab-case (wireless, datacom,
32	// terminals, cloud as of 0009). Validate the shape here; the DB FK + CHECK
33	// constraint enforces membership and the iff-rule against the actual tier.
34	export const ProductLineId = z
35	  .string()
36	  .trim()
37	  .min(1)
38	  .max(40)
39	  .regex(/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/, {
40	    message: "must be a kebab-case product-line id",
41	  })
42	export type ProductLineId = z.infer<typeof ProductLineId>
43	
44	// iff-rule helpers — the DB CHECK is the source of truth, but surfacing
45	// the rule at the validator layer turns a bad request into a typed Zod
46	// error instead of a generic 23514 from Postgres.
47	const requireProductLineIff = <T extends {
48	  tier?: OfficialTier
49	  requestedTier?: OfficialTier
50	  productLineId?: string | undefined
51	}>(
52	  v: T,
53	  tierKey: "tier" | "requestedTier",
54	): boolean => {
55	  const tier = v[tierKey]
56	  const hasPl = !!v.productLineId
57	  if (tier === "productLine") return hasPl
58	  if (tier === "company") return !hasPl
59	  return true
60	}

export type Locale = "en" | "zh"

export type Theme = "ivory" | "dark" | "mono"

export type ExtensionCategory = "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"
export type ExtensionScope = "personal" | "org" | "enterprise"
export type ExtensionBadge = "official" | "popular" | "new"
export type FuncCatKey = "workTask" | "business" | "tools"

export interface FuncTaxonomyL1 {
  key: string
  l2: string[]
}

export interface FuncTaxonomyNode {
  key: FuncCatKey
  l1: FuncTaxonomyL1[]
}

export interface TagLabel {
  en: string
  zh: string
}

export interface Department {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  children?: Department[]
}

export interface Extension {
  id: number
  dept: string
  tags: string[]
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  author: string
  category: ExtensionCategory
  badge: ExtensionBadge | null
  // Optional seed-side stamp. Lets the dev seed pre-approve a handful of
  // extensions so the new tier badge / filter rail has visible data on a
  // fresh DB. Production rows pick this up from the approval workflow,
  // never from this field.
  officialTier?: "productLine" | "company" | null
  // Required iff `officialTier === "productLine"` (the DB CHECK enforces
  // the iff-rule). The seed defaults to "wireless" when unset to keep
  // pre-existing rows visible without picking favourites.
  productLineId?: "wireless" | "datacom" | "terminals" | "cloud" | null
  downloads: number
  stars: number
  color: string
  icon: string
  funcCat: FuncCatKey
  subCat: string
  scope: ExtensionScope
  desc: string
  descZh: string
}

export interface CreatorFacet {
  id: string
  name: string | null
  email: string
  count: number
}

export interface PublisherFacet {
  id: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string | null
  slug: string
  count: number
}

export interface TagFacet {
  id: string
  labelEn: string
  labelZh: string
  count: number
}

export interface ProfileStats {
  installedCount: number
  publishedCount: number
  totalInstallsOfMine: number
  avgRatingOfMine: number | null
}

export interface ProfileInstalledRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  installedVersion: string
  installedAt: string
}

export interface ProfileSavedRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  savedAt: string
}

export interface ProfileDraftRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  updatedAt: string
  latestStatus: string | null
}

export interface ProfilePublishedRow {
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  latestVersion: string | null
  downloadsCount: number
  starsAvg: string
  ratingsCount: number
}

export type ProfileActivityEvent =
  | { kind: "installed"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; version: string }
  | { kind: "published"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; version: string }
  | { kind: "rated"; at: string; extensionId: string; slug: string; name: string; stars: number }

export interface ProfileRequestRow {
  requestId: string
  extensionId: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  category: string
  iconColor: string | null
  requestedTier: "productLine" | "company"
  subCat: string
  status: "pending" | "approved" | "rejected" | "withdrawn"
  reason: string | null
  reviewerNote: string | null
  createdAt: string
  decidedAt: string | null
}

export interface ProfileCollectionRow {
  id: string
  slug: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string | null
  description: string | null
  descriptionZh: string | null
  systemKind: "installed" | "saved" | null
  visibility: "private" | "public"
  itemCount: number
  updatedAt: string
}

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ChevronDown, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"
import type { RouteLocationRaw } from "vue-router"

const { t } = useI18n()
const route = useRoute()
const localePath = useLocalePath()

type CategoryKey = "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"

const PRIMARY_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "skills", labelKey: "sidebar.skills" },
  { key: "mcp", labelKey: "sidebar.mcpServers" },
]

const MORE_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "slash", labelKey: "sidebar.slashCommands" },
  { key: "plugins", labelKey: "sidebar.plugins" },
]

const localeExtensionsPath = computed(() => localePath("/extensions"))
const baseName = computed(() => (route.name?.toString() ?? "").split("___")[0] ?? "")

function hrefFor(key: CategoryKey): RouteLocationRaw {
  if (key === "mcp") return localePath("/mcp")
  if (key === "skills") return localePath("/skills")
  if (key === "slash") return localePath("/commands")
  if (key === "plugins") return localePath("/plugins")
  return { path: localeExtensionsPath.value, query: { category: key } }
}

function isCategoryActive(key: CategoryKey): boolean {
  if (key === "mcp") return baseName.value === "mcp" || baseName.value === "mcp-panorama"
  if (key === "skills") return baseName.value === "skills"
  if (key === "slash") return baseName.value === "commands"
  if (key === "plugins") return baseName.value === "plugins"
  if (route.path !== localeExtensionsPath.value) return false
  return route.query.category === key
}

const isMoreActive = computed(() => MORE_ITEMS.some(it => isCategoryActive(it.key)))
const moreOpen = ref(false)
</script>

<template>
  <div class="mx-auto w-full max-w-7xl h-full flex items-center gap-4 px-5">
    <NuxtLink
      :to="localePath('/')"
      class="font-serif text-lg tracking-tight hover:opacity-80"
    >
      AgentCenter
    </NuxtLink>

    <NuxtLink
      :to="localePath('/skills')"
      class="md:hidden text-sm text-(--color-ink-muted) hover:text-(--color-ink)"
    >
      {{ t("nav.browse") }}
    </NuxtLink>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ArrowUpRight, Search } from "lucide-vue-next"

type CategoryProp = "all" | "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"

const props = defineProps<{
  category: CategoryProp
}>()

const { t } = useI18n()
const route = useRoute()
const { filters, update } = useFilters()
const localePath = useLocalePath()

const fetchQuery = computed(() => {
  if (props.category === "all") return route.query
  return { ...route.query, category: props.category }
})

const { data, pending, refresh } = await useFetch("/api/internal/extensions", {
  query: fetchQuery,
  default: () => ({ items: [], total: 0, filters: {} }),
})

const { data: facets } = await useFetch("/api/internal/facets", {
  default: () => ({ creators: [], publishers: [], tags: [] }),
})

watch(() => route.fullPath, () => refresh())

const items = computed(() => data.value?.items ?? [])
const total = computed(() => data.value?.total ?? 0)
const query = computed(() => filters.value.q)
const filtersActive = computed(() => {
  const f = filters.value
  return Object.keys(f).some((k) => k !== "page" && f[k as keyof typeof f] !== undefined)
})

const searchPlaceholder = computed(() =>
  t(`extensions.category.${props.category}.searchPlaceholder`),
)
const categoryLabel = computed(() => t(`extensions.category.${props.category}.label`))

const canonicalPath = computed(() => {
  if (props.category === "all") return localePath("/extensions")
  if (props.category === "slash") return localePath("/commands")
  return localePath(`/${props.category}`)
})

const q = ref<string>(typeof route.query.q === "string" ? route.query.q : "")
let pushTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null

function pushQuery() {
  if (pushTimer) {
    clearTimeout(pushTimer)
    pushTimer = null
  }
  const trimmed = q.value.trim()
  update({ q: trimmed || undefined })
}

import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"

import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import {
  buildExtensionOrder,
  buildExtensionWhere,
} from "~~/shared/search/query"
import {
  PAGE_SIZE,
  pageOffset,
  type Filters,
} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

import type { Transactable } from "./types"

// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
// PGlite-backed integration test DB.

// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
// rename.
const listSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  officialTier: extensions.officialTier,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const detailSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  officialTier: extensions.officialTier,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
  permissions: extensions.permissions,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
  updatedAt: extensions.updatedAt,
  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
  visibility: extensions.visibility,
  productLineId: extensions.productLineId,
  revokedAt: extensions.revokedAt,
  revokedByUserId: extensions.revokedByUserId,
  revocationNote: extensions.revocationNote,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const relatedSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  officialTier: extensions.officialTier,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
}

export async function findManyForList(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
) {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)

  return db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(...order)
    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
}

export async function countFiltered(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
): Promise<number> {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const [row] = await db
    .select({ count: count() })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(where)
  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
}

export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  //
  // Both queries cap to one row; without a stable tiebreaker the choice
  // would flap on ties (two curated rows published at the same instant,
  // or two extensions with identical download counts). Sort by id last so
  // the home hero is deterministic across requests.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(
      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
      desc(extensions.id),
    )
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AgentCenter Extension Manifest Spec

Every extension bundle (`.zip`) **must** contain a `manifest.toml` at the archive root.
The manifest declares identity, discoverability, compatibility, and per-agent install targets.

---

## Top-level sections

### `[extension]` — required

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `slug` | string | ✓ | URL-safe identifier, unique across the registry. Pattern: `[a-z0-9-]+`, max 64 chars. |
| `name` | string | ✓ | Display name in English. |
| `nameZh` | string | | Display name in Chinese (optional bilingual). |
| `version` | string | ✓ | Semver (e.g. `1.0.0`). |
| `category` | enum | ✓ | One of `skills`, `mcp`, `slash`, `plugins`. |
| `scope` | enum | ✓ | Audience: `personal`, `org`, `enterprise`. |
| `license` | string | | SPDX identifier (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`). Omit for proprietary. |
| `description` | string | ✓ | Short description in English, max 280 chars. |
| `descriptionZh` | string | | Short description in Chinese. |
| `tagline` | string | | One-line tagline shown in the featured banner. |
| `tags` | string[] | | Tag slugs from the registry vocabulary (see Tags below). |

### `[categorization]` — required

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `funcCat` | enum | Functional category: `workTask`, `business`, `tools`. |
| `subCat` | string | L1 subcategory key (e.g. `softDev`, `systemDesign`). |
| `l2` | string | L2 sub-subcategory key (e.g. `backend`, `frontend`). Optional. |

### `[links]` — optional

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `homepage` | URL | Public homepage or docs site. |
| `repo` | URL | Source repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc.). |

### `[compatibility]` — optional

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `minAgentVersion` | string | Minimum agent version required (semver, e.g. `0.8.0`). |
| `os` | string[] | Restrict to OS list: `darwin`, `linux`, `windows`. Omit = all. |

### `[install.<agent>]` — at least one required

Declares per-agent install destinations. The CLI reads the profile that matches
`agentcenter config get agent` (default: `claude`).

**Skills** (`category = "skills"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
skills = "~/.claude/skills/{slug}"
```

**MCP Servers** (`category = "mcp"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
mcpConfig = "~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
mcpKey    = "{slug}"
```

**Slash Commands** (`category = "slash"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
commands = "~/.claude/commands/{slug}.md"
```

**Plugins** (`category = "plugins"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
plugins = "~/.claude/plugins/{slug}"
```

Path tokens:

| Token | Expands to |
|---|---|
| `{slug}` | Extension slug |
| `{version}` | Installed version |
| `{agent}` | Active agent profile name |

Optional `[install.<agent>.postInstall]`:

```toml
[install.claude.postInstall]
message = "Set your API key: agentcenter config set {slug}.apiKey <key>"
```

---

## Full example

```toml
[extension]
slug        = "web-search-pro"
name        = "Web Search Pro"
nameZh      = "网页搜索增强"
version     = "1.2.0"
category    = "skills"
scope       = "org"
license     = "MIT"
description = "Real-time web search integration for Claude via the Brave Search API."
descriptionZh = "为 Claude 接入 Brave 搜索 API，提供实时网页搜索能力。"
tagline     = "Search the web, right inside Claude."
tags        = ["search", "real-time", "stable"]

[categorization]
funcCat = "workTask"
subCat  = "softDev"
l2      = "backend"

[links]
homepage = "https://example.com/web-search-pro"
repo     = "https://github.com/example/web-search-pro"

[compatibility]
minAgentVersion = "0.8.0"
os = ["darwin", "linux"]

[install.claude]
skills = "~/.claude/skills/{slug}"

[install.claude.postInstall]
message = "Add your Brave API key: agentcenter config set web-search-pro.apiKey <your-key>"
```

---

## Bundle structure

```text
web-search-pro-1.2.0.zip
├── manifest.toml       ← required, at archive root
├── skill.md            ← skill definition (for category=skills)
├── README.md           ← optional, rendered on detail page
└── assets/             ← optional supporting files
```

For `category = mcp`, include a `server.js` / `server.py` or equivalent binary.
For `category = slash`, include the command markdown file(s).
For `category = plugins`, include the plugin entry point per agent conventions.

---

## Tags vocabulary

Tags are defined in `shared/tags.ts`. Current set (32 tags):

`ai-native` `analytics` `automation` `ci-cd` `cloud` `code-gen` `code-review`
`data` `deployment` `devops` `document` `embedded` `enterprise` `grpc`
`http` `infra` `integration` `llm` `monitoring` `mqtt` `network` `open-source`
`performance` `personal` `real-time` `search` `security` `stable` `testing`
`translation` `ui` `version-control`

---

## Versioning rules

- Versions must be valid semver.
- Re-publishing the same `slug@version` is rejected (immutable releases).
- A new `version` is required for every update.
- The registry always serves the **latest published** version at `GET /api/v1/extensions/:slug`.
  Future `/api/v1/extensions/:slug/versions` endpoint will list history.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "feat(p10-cli-type): add CLI as a new extension type" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Adds `cli` as a fifth `extension_category` value alongside `skills` / `mcp` / `slash` / `plugins`. CLI extensions are publisher-uploaded bundles that the Agent CLI installs to `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` — a directory drop with the same rollback semantics as `skills`. The contents may be an executable, an installable package (npm/uv), or both; PATH wiring or package-manager invocation is handled by the publisher's `[install.<agent>.postInstall].message`, not the installer.

The change is additive to the frozen `/api/v1` contract — older CLI clients see an unknown `category` value but no existing field shape changes.

## Commits

1. **`feat(p10-cli-type): add cli extension category to schema and validators`** — schema enum + Drizzle ALTER TYPE migration (`0013_nostalgic_slapstick.sql`) + every type-level single source of truth widened (`shared/types.ts`, `shared/validators/manifest.ts`, `shared/validators/filters.ts`, `shared/validators/approvals.ts`, `shared/data/approval-admins.ts`) + the five local enum duplicates flushed out by typecheck (`server/repositories/extensions.ts:findRelated`, `app/components/extension/ExtensionListing.vue`, `app/components/approvals/ReviewerMatrix.vue` + its tab array, `app/pages/admin/approvals/index.vue`, `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` + its `hrefFor` / `isCategoryActive` / `MORE_ITEMS`).
2. **`feat(p10-cli-type): wire cli installer and manifest spec`** — `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` mirroring `skills.ts` (target `~/.claude/cli/{slug}`), `installExtension`/`uninstallExtension` switches routed to it, and a `[install.claude] cli = "~/.claude/cli/{slug}"` example added to `docs/manifest-spec.md` with a note on the post-install hand-off.
3. **`feat(p10-cli-type): seed and surface cli category in UI`** — `legacy-categories.global.ts` routes `?category=cli` → `/cli`; new `app/pages/cli/index.vue` mirrors `mcp/index.vue`; `publish.defaultClassification()` maps `cli` to `tools/cli`; approval-admin seed gets one company-tier All + two macros (softDev → ben, cloud → cory) + a PL wireless All so the new ext-type tab in `/admin/approvals` isn't visibly empty; i18n EN/ZH labels for `extensions.category.cli`, `sidebar.cli`, `publish.options.category.cli`, and the admin matrix `cat.cli`.

## Notes

- Tech-debt observation, not addressed here: the `ExtensionCategory` enum is duplicated in **eleven** places (one canonical type + one Drizzle pgEnum + four validator z.enums + five local-component duplicates). Worth consolidating into a single shared const in a follow-up — flag for ADR-style discussion since the consolidation crosses the shared/server/app/cli boundary.
- I picked `tools/cli` as the default `subCat`; happy to drop the case so `cli` falls through to `tools/general` if you prefer.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run validate` clean locally (typecheck + lint + 394 tests pass)
- [ ] `bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate` on a clean Postgres — `ALTER TYPE` applies without diff drift
- [ ] `SEED_PASSWORD=test bun run db:seed` — new `cli` admin rows insert
- [ ] Visit `/cli` and `/zh/cli` — listing renders with the i18n label
- [ ] Sign in as amy and visit `/admin/approvals` — `CLI` tab appears with seeded admins
- [ ] Publish wizard end-to-end with `category: "cli"`; draft → scanning → ready
- [ ] `cd cli && bun run build && ./dist/bin/agentcenter install <slug>` against the dev server — files land in `~/.claude/cli/<slug>/`; `agentcenter uninstall <slug>` clears it
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
PR opened: **https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt/pull/52**

Three commits on `feat/cli-extension-type`, all green locally (typecheck + lint + 394 tests). Summary of what landed:

- **Commit 1** — enum widened to include `"cli"` in every single source of truth (Drizzle enum + 5 validator/type/seed copies) plus the 5 local component duplicates that typecheck flushed out; migration `0013_nostalgic_slapstick.sql` is a single `ALTER TYPE … ADD VALUE 'cli'`.
- **Commit 2** — new `cli/src/installers/cli.ts` targeting `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` (mirrors `skills.ts`), wired into the install/uninstall switches, and a `[install.<agent>] cli = …` example added to the manifest spec.
- **Commit 3** — listing route `/cli`, legacy-category redirect, `defaultClassification` maps `cli → tools/cli`, approval-admin seed rows for the new tab, i18n labels in EN + ZH.

Two things called out in the PR body:
1. The `ExtensionCategory` enum is now duplicated in 11 places — flagged as a follow-up consolidation, not addressed in this PR.

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough This PR adds a new "cli" extension category throughout the codebase. The feature extends the type system and database schema, implements CLI installation logic, adds frontend navigation and components, and includes localized labels and documentation. ## Changes **CLI Extension Category Support** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Type System & Contract**
`shared/types.ts`, `shared/db/schema/extension.ts`, `shared/validators/approvals.ts`, `shared/validators/manifest.ts`, `shared/validators/filters.ts` | `ExtensionCategory` type and all derived Zod validators and database enum are extended to include `"cli"` as a valid category value, establishing the contract across all validation and persistence layers. | | **Database Migration**
`drizzle/0013_nostalgic_slapstick.sql`, `drizzle/meta/_journal.json` | PostgreSQL enum migration adds `'cli'` value to `public.extension_category` type and records the schema version in the Drizzle journal. | | **Server-side Routing & Admin Data**
`server/repositories/extensions.ts`, `server/utils/publish.ts`, `shared/data/approval-admins.ts` | `findRelated` query accepts `"cli"` category; `defaultClassification` maps `"cli"` to `funcCat: "tools"` and `subCat: "cli"`; approval admin seeding adds company-tier and per-category "cli" rows for non-empty matrix rendering. | | **CLI Installation & Uninstallation**
`cli/src/installers/cli.ts`, `cli/src/installers/index.ts` | New module implements `installCli`, `uninstallCli`, and `listInstalledCliSlugs` utilities that manage per-user CLI directory at `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` with zip extraction, error cleanup, and directory discovery; installer index routes `"cli"` category extensions to these handlers. | | **Frontend Navigation & Components**
`app/components/layout/TopBar.vue`, `app/components/approvals/ReviewerMatrix.vue`, `app/components/extension/ExtensionListing.vue`, `app/pages/cli/index.vue`, `app/middleware/legacy-categories.global.ts`, `app/pages/admin/approvals/index.vue` | TopBar adds "cli" category to navigation dropdown with locale-aware `/cli` routing and active-state checks; ReviewerMatrix and ExtensionListing props accept `"cli"`; new `/cli` index page renders `ExtensionListing` filtered to "cli" category; category redirects and admin approvals extend typing. | | **Translations & Documentation**
`i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json`, `docs/manifest-spec.md`, `shared/validators/approvals.test.ts` | English and Chinese locales add "CLI" / "命令行工具" labels in category selectors, sidebar, and admin reviewer matrix; manifest spec documents `category = "cli"` installation target path `~/.claude/cli/{slug}` and clarifies installation is a directory drop with PATH/package-manager wiring left to publisher/user instructions; test value changed from `"cli"` to `"not-a-real-category"` for unknown-category rejection. | ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~22 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt#2](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt/pull/2): Both PRs modify the same `ExtensionCategory` type union; the related PR initially defines the base type without `"cli"`, while this PR extends it to include the new category. - [ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt#32](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt/pull/32): Both PRs modify `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue`'s category routing logic; the related PR refactors navigation structure, while this PR extends it to support the new `"cli"` category. - [ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt#6](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt/pull/6): Both PRs touch `shared/validators/manifest.ts`; the related PR introduces manifest Zod schemas used by publishing, while this PR extends those schemas' `category` enum to accept `"cli"`. ## Poem > 🐰 A CLI category hops into view, > From types to routes to installers too, > Database seeds the matrix with care, > Translations echo everywhere, > Now CLI tools can freely share! 🎉

All three findings verified. Breakdown:

| # | Severity | Location | Real? | In scope for this PR? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 🔴 Critical | `cli/src/installers/cli.ts:10,19,33` — path traversal: `slug` and zip entry paths aren't validated, so `../` escapes `~/.claude/cli` | Yes | **Pre-existing** — `skills.ts:10,19,27` has the identical bug. I copied it verbatim. |
| 2 | 🟠 Major | `ReviewerMatrix.vue:79` — `hash.match(/#cat=(skills\|mcp\|slash\|plugins)/)` excludes `cli`, so `#cat=cli` falls back to `"skills"` | Yes | Yes — introduced here, missed by my grep (regex alternation didn't match my quoted-string search pattern) |
| 3 | 🟡 Minor | `docs/manifest-spec.md:18` — category enum description still lists only the four old values | Yes | Yes — I updated the install-destination subsection but missed the field-description row |

I'll fix #2 and #3 now since they're clearly this PR's scope and trivial. For #1 — it's a real critical bug but it's pre-existing in `skills.ts`. Which path do you prefer?

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(cli-installers): validate slug and zip entry paths to prevent traversal

A malicious zip with an entry like `../../etc/foo` would have escaped
the installer's intended root directory; same for a crafted slug. The
new safe-paths helper rejects invalid slugs at the entrypoint and
resolves every zip entry inside the destination dir, throwing if the
resolved path escapes. Both installers (skills and cli) route through
the same helper so the fix can't drift between them.

Pre-existing in skills.ts; the bug just became more visible after
cli.ts copied the same shape. Flagged on PR #52 by CodeRabbit.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AgentCenter Extension Manifest Spec

Every extension bundle (`.zip`) **must** contain a `manifest.toml` at the archive root.
The manifest declares identity, discoverability, compatibility, and per-agent install targets.

---

## Top-level sections

### `[extension]` — required

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `slug` | string | ✓ | URL-safe identifier, unique across the registry. Pattern: `[a-z0-9-]+`, max 64 chars. |
| `name` | string | ✓ | Display name in English. |
| `nameZh` | string | | Display name in Chinese (optional bilingual). |
| `version` | string | ✓ | Semver (e.g. `1.0.0`). |
| `category` | enum | ✓ | One of `skills`, `mcp`, `slash`, `plugins`. |
| `scope` | enum | ✓ | Audience: `personal`, `org`, `enterprise`. |
| `license` | string | | SPDX identifier (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`). Omit for proprietary. |
| `description` | string | ✓ | Short description in English, max 280 chars. |
| `descriptionZh` | string | | Short description in Chinese. |
| `tagline` | string | | One-line tagline shown in the featured banner. |
| `tags` | string[] | | Tag slugs from the registry vocabulary (see Tags below). |

### `[categorization]` — required

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `funcCat` | enum | Functional category: `workTask`, `business`, `tools`. |
| `subCat` | string | L1 subcategory key (e.g. `softDev`, `systemDesign`). |
| `l2` | string | L2 sub-subcategory key (e.g. `backend`, `frontend`). Optional. |

### `[links]` — optional

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `homepage` | URL | Public homepage or docs site. |
| `repo` | URL | Source repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc.). |

### `[compatibility]` — optional

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `minAgentVersion` | string | Minimum agent version required (semver, e.g. `0.8.0`). |
| `os` | string[] | Restrict to OS list: `darwin`, `linux`, `windows`. Omit = all. |

### `[install.<agent>]` — at least one required

Declares per-agent install destinations. The CLI reads the profile that matches
`agentcenter config get agent` (default: `claude`).

**Skills** (`category = "skills"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
skills = "~/.claude/skills/{slug}"
```

**MCP Servers** (`category = "mcp"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
mcpConfig = "~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
mcpKey    = "{slug}"
```

**Slash Commands** (`category = "slash"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
commands = "~/.claude/commands/{slug}.md"
```

**Plugins** (`category = "plugins"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
plugins = "~/.claude/plugins/{slug}"
```

**CLI tools** (`category = "cli"`):

```toml
[install.claude]
cli = "~/.claude/cli/{slug}"
```

The CLI directory may contain an executable, an installable package
(e.g. an `npm`/`uv` project), or both. The installer does a directory
drop — wiring the contents into `PATH` or running a package manager
is the user's job, and publishers should use
`[install.claude.postInstall].message` to spell that out.

Path tokens:

| Token | Expands to |
|---|---|
| `{slug}` | Extension slug |
| `{version}` | Installed version |
| `{agent}` | Active agent profile name |

Optional `[install.<agent>.postInstall]`:

```toml
[install.claude.postInstall]
message = "Set your API key: agentcenter config set {slug}.apiKey <key>"
```

---

## Full example

```toml
[extension]
slug        = "web-search-pro"
name        = "Web Search Pro"
nameZh      = "网页搜索增强"
version     = "1.2.0"
category    = "skills"
scope       = "org"
license     = "MIT"
description = "Real-time web search integration for Claude via the Brave Search API."
descriptionZh = "为 Claude 接入 Brave 搜索 API，提供实时网页搜索能力。"
tagline     = "Search the web, right inside Claude."
tags        = ["search", "real-time", "stable"]

[categorization]
funcCat = "workTask"
subCat  = "softDev"
l2      = "backend"

[links]
homepage = "https://example.com/web-search-pro"
repo     = "https://github.com/example/web-search-pro"

[compatibility]
minAgentVersion = "0.8.0"
os = ["darwin", "linux"]

[install.claude]
skills = "~/.claude/skills/{slug}"

[install.claude.postInstall]
message = "Add your Brave API key: agentcenter config set web-search-pro.apiKey <your-key>"
```

---

## Bundle structure

```text
web-search-pro-1.2.0.zip
├── manifest.toml       ← required, at archive root
├── skill.md            ← skill definition (for category=skills)
├── README.md           ← optional, rendered on detail page
└── assets/             ← optional supporting files
```

For `category = mcp`, include a `server.js` / `server.py` or equivalent binary.
For `category = slash`, include the command markdown file(s).
For `category = plugins`, include the plugin entry point per agent conventions.

---

## Tags vocabulary

Tags are defined in `shared/tags.ts`. Current set (32 tags):

`ai-native` `analytics` `automation` `ci-cd` `cloud` `code-gen` `code-review`
`data` `deployment` `devops` `document` `embedded` `enterprise` `grpc`
`http` `infra` `integration` `llm` `monitoring` `mqtt` `network` `open-source`
`performance` `personal` `real-time` `search` `security` `stable` `testing`
`translation` `ui` `version-control`

---

## Versioning rules

- Versions must be valid semver.
- Re-publishing the same `slug@version` is rejected (immutable releases).
- A new `version` is required for every update.
- The registry always serves the **latest published** version at `GET /api/v1/extensions/:slug`.
  Future `/api/v1/extensions/:slug/versions` endpoint will list history.

lines: | `nameZh` | string | | Display name in Chinese (optional bilingual). |
| `license` | string | | SPDX identifier (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`). Omit for proprietary. |
| `description` | string | ✓ | Short description in English, max 280 chars. |

file.content:
import type { Locale, TagLabel } from "~~/shared/types"

export const TAG_LABELS: Record<string, TagLabel> = {
  search: { en: "search", zh: "搜索" },
  "real-time": { en: "real-time", zh: "实时" },
  stable: { en: "stable", zh: "稳定版" },
  beta: { en: "beta", zh: "测试版" },
  official: { en: "official", zh: "官方" },
  python: { en: "python", zh: "Python" },
  sandbox: { en: "sandbox", zh: "沙箱" },
  api: { en: "api", zh: "API" },
  vcs: { en: "vcs", zh: "版本控制" },
  docs: { en: "docs", zh: "文档" },
  sync: { en: "sync", zh: "同步" },
  integration: { en: "integration", zh: "集成" },
  summarize: { en: "summarize", zh: "摘要" },
  vision: { en: "vision", zh: "视觉" },
  ocr: { en: "ocr", zh: "OCR" },
  messaging: { en: "messaging", zh: "消息" },
  i18n: { en: "i18n", zh: "国际化" },
  calendar: { en: "calendar", zh: "日历" },
  data: { en: "data", zh: "数据" },
  charts: { en: "charts", zh: "图表" },
  analytics: { en: "analytics", zh: "分析" },
  sql: { en: "sql", zh: "SQL" },
  database: { en: "database", zh: "数据库" },
  code: { en: "code", zh: "代码" },
  explain: { en: "explain", zh: "解释" },
  architecture: { en: "architecture", zh: "架构" },
  review: { en: "review", zh: "评审" },
  devops: { en: "devops", zh: "DevOps" },
  cloud: { en: "cloud", zh: "云" },
  testing: { en: "testing", zh: "测试" },
  automation: { en: "automation", zh: "自动化" },
  iot: { en: "iot", zh: "物联网" },
}

export function tagLabel(key: string, locale: Locale): string {
  return TAG_LABELS[key]?.[locale] ?? key
}

content.text:
Six commits on the branch, three ahead of remote. To see the new CLI extensions:

```bash
SEED_PASSWORD=test bun run db:migrate && SEED_PASSWORD=test bun run db:seed
bun run dev
```

Then visit `/cli` — you should see **kubectl-explain** (Company-Official, cloud), **log-tail** (Popular, cloud), and **pr-summarize** (New, vcs). The "More" menu in the top bar now has a **CLI** entry. Sign in as `amy@agentcenter.dev` and visit `/admin/approvals` to see the new CLI tab populated with the seeded reviewers.

content.content:
1	# Deployment
2	
3	AgentCenter (Nuxt) ships as a standalone Node server, with Postgres + an object store + Inngest behind it. Three deploy targets are supported:
4	
5	- **Node** (default) — `nitro: { preset: "node-server" }`. Runs anywhere Node 22+ runs: bare metal, container, PaaS.
6	- **Vercel** (auto-detected) — Nitro picks `preset: "vercel"` when `process.env.VERCEL` is set. The `vercel-build` script in `package.json` chains `drizzle-kit migrate && bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts && bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts && bun scripts/seed-catalog.ts && nuxt build`, so every deploy: (1) applies pending migrations against `DATABASE_URL`, then (2) idempotently upserts the static MCP panorama landscape, editorial collections, and catalog marketplace stubs, then (3) builds. Aborts the deploy if any step fails (prevents code shipping against a stale or empty schema). The destructive `db:seed` script (demo users + sample extensions) is **not** in the build path — that stays a one-shot manual operation.
7	- **Cloudflare** (stretch) — `nitro: { preset: "cloudflare_module" }`. Workers + R2 binding. Not maintained alongside primary; expect bundle-size + driver tweaks.
8	
9	This guide covers the Node path. Vercel needs only the `DATABASE_URL` env var set in the project (Build & Runtime). The Cloudflare path is a config flip plus a storage driver swap.
10	
11	## Prerequisites
12	
13	| Service | Purpose | Required |
14	|---|---|---|
15	| **PostgreSQL 15+** | Application database | yes |
16	| **Object storage** — Supabase Storage **or** Cloudflare R2 | Extension bundles | yes |
17	| **Inngest** account (cloud) or local dev server | Background jobs (scan, index) | yes (cloud for prod) |
18	| **Node 22+** | Runtime | yes |
19	| **Bun 1.3.12** | Build (production runs Node, not Bun) | yes during build |
20	
21	## 1. Provision the database
22	
23	Any Postgres works — Supabase, Neon, AWS RDS, self-hosted. Apply migrations:
24	
25	```bash
26	bun install
27	DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..." bun run db:migrate
28	DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..." bun run db:apply-fts
29	DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..." bun run db:seed   # optional: chains seed.ts + seed-catalog.ts (16 demo + 66 catalog)
30	```
31	
32	The `db:apply-fts` step requires `psql` on PATH. It runs `drizzle/0002_fts_search_vector.sql` against your DB (FTS column + trgm indexes).
33	
34	### Seeding super-admins and reviewer matrix
35	
36	The approval workflow needs at least one super-admin so the
37	`/admin/reviewers` matrix is reachable. `bun run db:seed` promotes
38	one of the seeded `CREATORS` users to `role = 'superAdmin'`:
39	
40	```bash
41	SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL="amy@agentcenter.dev" bun run db:seed
42	```
43	
44	`SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_EMAIL` defaults to `amy@agentcenter.dev` (the first
45	creator in `scripts/seed.ts`). Supply any email from `CREATORS` to
46	pick a different super-admin; an unknown email logs a warning and
47	seeds no super-admin (the matrix UI then 403s for every user — fix
48	by re-running with a valid email).
49	
50	The same seed populates the admin matrix from
51	`shared/data/approval-admins.ts` so every demo extension type tab
52	has covering admins (All / macro / micro across Company and the
53	product lines) on a fresh DB.
54	
55	`db:seed` is destructive (TRUNCATE … CASCADE on orgs and tags) and
56	is the "reset to demo state" command. Production deploys use only
57	the idempotent seeds in the `vercel-build` chain.
58	
59	To prevent typos from wiping real data, `db:seed` refuses to run
60	against a non-localhost `DATABASE_URL` unless `SEED_ALLOW_REMOTE=1`
61	is set. See the staging workflow below; **do not set this flag
62	against a production environment**.
63	
64	### Seeding a staging Vercel environment for demos
65	
66	If your Vercel deployment is a staging / demo environment (separate
67	project or separate database from real prod), you can plant the full
68	demo dataset there so sign-in works and the admin surfaces are
69	populated.
70	
71	1. **Link the local repo** to the Vercel project (one-time):
72	
73	   ```bash
74	   vercel link
75	   ```
76	
77	2. **Pull the env for the right environment** into a local file:
78	
79	   ```bash
80	   vercel env pull .env.staging --environment=<env>
81	   ```
82	
83	   Pick `<env>` based on your project's shape:
84	
85	   - **Single-environment project** (the only deployed URL is the
86	     Vercel `production` env, no real prod users yet) → use
87	     `--environment=production`. The demo site IS the production
88	     environment; that's where the seed needs to land.
89	   - **Separate staging Vercel project** (e.g. `agentcenter-staging`
90	     distinct from `agentcenter-prod`) → use `--environment=production`
91	     against the staging project. Same logic — its `production` is
92	     your demo.
93	   - **Single project hosting both real prod and a preview branch**
94	     → use `--environment=preview`. `production` here means the live
95	     site with real users; do not seed it.
96	
97	   When in doubt, run `vercel env ls` to list environments and confirm
98	   what's there before pulling. The `SEED_ALLOW_REMOTE=1` guardrail in
99	   step 5 is a checkpoint, not a substitute for knowing which DB the
100	   pulled `DATABASE_URL` actually points at.
101	
102	3. **Export into the shell**:
103	
104	   ```bash
105	   set -a; source .env.staging; set +a
106	   ```
107	
108	4. **Confirm the target host** before the destructive step:
109	
110	   ```bash
111	   echo "$DATABASE_URL" | grep -oE '@[^/]+'
112	   ```
113	
114	   Read the hostname aloud. If it looks like prod, stop.
115	
116	5. **Run the seed**:
117	
118	   ```bash
119	   SEED_PASSWORD=demo SEED_ALLOW_REMOTE=1 bun run db:seed
120	   ```
121	
122	   `SEED_ALLOW_REMOTE=1` is the explicit acknowledgement that you
123	   know the URL points somewhere remote. Without it, the script
124	   bails before connecting.
125	
126	6. **Sign in** at `https://<your-staging-host>/sign-in` as
127	   `amy@agentcenter.dev` with the password from step 5. The
128	   UserButton dropdown will surface "Approval queue" and "Reviewer
129	   matrix" entries (super-admin only).
130	
131	7. **Rotate the demo password** any time by re-running step 5 with
132	   a different `SEED_PASSWORD`. The seed is idempotent — re-runs
133	   reset the DB to a known demo state and rotate all 6 demo users'
134	   passwords in one shot.
135	
136	8. **Don't commit `.env.staging`** — `.gitignore` already covers
137	   `*.env*`, but `git status` after step 2 is a worthwhile habit.
138	
139	`vercel-build` runs on every subsequent deploy and only touches the
140	idempotent seeds (mcp-landscape, editorial-collections, catalog) —
141	the demo creators, extensions, and approval-reviewer matrix planted
142	by step 5 survive across deploys.
143	
144	## 2. Provision object storage
145	
146	### Supabase Storage (default)
147	
148	1. Supabase dashboard → Storage → New bucket (e.g. `agentcenter-bundles`)
149	2. Generate a service-role key (Settings → API → service_role secret)
150	3. Set env: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `SUPABASE_BUCKET`
151	
152	### Cloudflare R2 (alternative)
153	
154	1. Cloudflare dashboard → R2 → Create bucket
155	2. Create an API token with Object Read & Write on the bucket
156	3. Set env: `R2_ACCOUNT_ID`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `R2_BUCKET`
157	4. Apply CORS so browsers can `PUT` direct to R2 (the upload-sign endpoint hands out presigned URLs; the browser uploads without proxying through the app)
158	
159	`R2_ACCOUNT_ID` being set picks R2 over Supabase automatically. See `server/utils/storage.ts`.
160	
161	## 3. Inngest
162	
163	1. Create an Inngest app (cloud) and copy `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` + `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY`
164	2. After deploying, register the webhook URL in the Inngest dashboard:
165	   ```text
166	   https://yourdomain.com/api/inngest
167	   ```
168	3. Inngest will verify by calling the endpoint; `scan-bundle` and `reindex-search` will appear as registered functions
169	
170	For local dev, run the Inngest dev server instead:
171	```bash
172	bunx inngest-cli@1.19.2 dev
173	```
174	It auto-discovers `/api/inngest` and routes events locally. No event/signing keys needed locally.
175	
176	## 4. Build & run
177	
178	```bash
179	bun install --frozen-lockfile
180	bun run build
181	PORT=3000 node .output/server/index.mjs
182	```
183	
184	Front the Node process with a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy) for TLS, gzip, and HTTP/2.
185	
186	### Required env vars at runtime
187	
188	| Variable | Purpose |
189	|---|---|
190	| `DATABASE_URL` | Postgres connection string |
191	| `BETTER_AUTH_URL` | Public origin (e.g. `https://agentcenter.example.com`) |
192	| `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` | Random 32+ char string (`openssl rand -base64 32`) |
193	| `NUXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` | Public origin (same as `BETTER_AUTH_URL`) |
194	| `NODE_ENV=production` | Enables prod cookies, prod Inngest routing, etc. |
195	| `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` | Inngest cloud event key |
196	| `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY` | Inngest cloud webhook verification key |
197	| `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` + `SUPABASE_BUCKET` | Storage (if using Supabase) |
198	| `R2_ACCOUNT_ID` + `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` + `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` + `R2_BUCKET` | Storage (if using R2) |
199	
200	See `.env.example` for the canonical list.
201	
202	## 5. Health checks
203	
204	After the server is running:
205	
206	- `curl https://yourdomain.com/api/v1/extensions` → 200 with `{ items, total, page, pageSize }`
207	- `curl -I https://yourdomain.com/` → 302 to `/en`
208	- `curl https://yourdomain.com/en/extensions` → 200 with the browse page HTML
209	- Sign-up via the UI → land on `/en/onboard` → pick a department → land on `/en/`
210	- Publish a test bundle (zip with valid `manifest.toml`) via `/en/publish/new` → scope=personal → Inngest scans → row appears on `/en/extensions`
211	- Build the CLI binary from `../agentcenter/cli/`, point at production URL, run `agentcenter login` end-to-end through the device-code flow
212	
213	## 6. Upgrades
214	
215	Migrations are idempotent (Drizzle journal-based). For zero-downtime upgrades:
216	
217	1. Apply new migrations against the live DB before swapping app binaries
218	2. Run the FTS migration once (only re-run if `drizzle/0002_fts_search_vector.sql` was changed)
219	3. Hot-swap the Node process behind the reverse proxy (PM2, systemd, blue/green)
220	
221	## 7. Cloudflare deploy (stretch)
222	
223	```ts
224	// nuxt.config.ts
225	nitro: { preset: "cloudflare_module" }
226	```
227	
228	Plus:
229	- Swap `drizzle-orm/postgres-js` (TCP) for `@neondatabase/serverless` (HTTP) or Hyperdrive — Workers can't open raw TCP
230	- Use the R2 binding (`event.context.cloudflare.env.BUCKET`) instead of the S3 SDK; cuts bundle size significantly
231	- `wrangler secret put` for each runtime env var
232	
233	Both swaps are localized to `server/utils/db.ts` and `server/utils/storage.ts`. Everything else is preset-agnostic.
234	
235	This path is documented but not maintained alongside the Node path. If you need it, expect a half-day of bundle-size triage on first deploy.
